stocks.name: IBM Corporation
stocks.explanation: IBM issued a preliminary Q2 warning, disclosing roughly $17.2B in revenue and $2.93 adjusted EPS, both short of its internal plan and Street consensus. Infrastructure revenue fell 7% on weak mainframe Z sales and attached transaction-processing software, while CEO Arvind Krishna cited clients redirecting spend toward servers, storage and memory ahead of supply constraints, plus large deals slipping past quarter-end. The magnitude of the miss drove the stock's steepest single-day drop in years. Final results and the earnings call are set for July 22.

stocks.name: ZoomInfo Technologies
stocks.explanation: ZoomInfo rallied as investors rotated into deeply oversold enterprise-software names, shifting capital from the AI infrastructure layer toward application-layer software that monetizes AI. The go-to-market data provider has been one of the sector's worst performers this year, leaving it primed for a sharp bounce. The move extended a multi-day recovery in beaten-down SaaS names.

stocks.name: Okta Inc.
stocks.explanation: Okta climbed alongside a broad cybersecurity rally sparked by a cluster of Scotiabank upgrades targeting identity and exposure-management vendors. As a leading identity provider, Okta was a direct beneficiary of the positive read-through. Cooler-than-expected June CPI added a macro tailwind for high-multiple software.

stocks.name: CrowdStrike Holdings
stocks.explanation: CrowdStrike surged as the cybersecurity complex rallied on Scotiabank analyst upgrades and a rotation back into software from semiconductors. Already up sharply year to date, the stock extended gains as investors favored recurring-revenue security leaders. Cooler inflation data reinforced the bid for growth software.

stocks.name: Zscaler Inc.
stocks.explanation: Zscaler advanced amid the sector-wide cybersecurity rally driven by analyst upgrades across identity and exposure-management names. The cloud-security vendor benefited from the positive read-through and the broader rotation into enterprise software. June CPI relief supported richly valued growth names.

stocks.name: Tenable Holdings
stocks.explanation: Tenable rose as exposure-management and vulnerability vendors were specifically flagged in Scotiabank's cybersecurity upgrade cycle. As a core exposure-management play, Tenable was among the more direct beneficiaries. The move rode the broader software rotation and inflation relief.

stocks.name: DigitalOcean Holdings
stocks.explanation: DigitalOcean jumped as investors rotated into infrastructure- and application-software names amid profit-taking in AI chips. The cloud provider aimed at smaller businesses caught a bid alongside the broader SaaS recovery. Cooler CPI added support for higher-multiple tech.

stocks.name: Rapid7 Inc.
stocks.explanation: Rapid7 climbed as part of the cybersecurity rally tied to Scotiabank's upgrades across the exposure- and vulnerability-management space. The security-analytics vendor benefited from the sector read-through. Its low absolute share price amplified the percentage move.

stocks.name: Monolithic Power Systems
stocks.explanation: Monolithic Power Systems rebounded with the broader semiconductor group after Monday's chip-led selloff, helped by cooler June CPI and firm demand for power semiconductors tied to AI servers. The analog power-chip maker remains a favored AI-infrastructure supplier despite a rich ~90x P/E. The bounce recovered part of the prior session's decline.

stocks.name: SentinelOne Inc.
stocks.explanation: SentinelOne rallied with the cybersecurity complex on Scotiabank's identity- and exposure-management upgrades and the rotation into software. As an AI-native endpoint-security vendor, it benefited from renewed sector enthusiasm. Inflation relief supported the group.

stocks.name: NetApp Inc.
stocks.explanation: NetApp gained as enterprise storage names caught a bid amid IBM's commentary that clients are front-loading purchases of servers, storage and memory ahead of expected price increases. The data-storage vendor read as a relative beneficiary of that spending shift. CPI relief and a semiconductor bounce added support.

stocks.name: Varonis Systems
stocks.explanation: Varonis rose alongside the cybersecurity rally, benefiting from Scotiabank's upgrades across data-security and exposure-management vendors. The data-security specialist tracked the sector's read-through higher. Rotation into software and cooler inflation reinforced the move.

stocks.name: Dell Technologies
stocks.explanation: Dell advanced on the same dynamic IBM flagged: enterprise clients accelerating purchases of servers, storage and memory ahead of anticipated component price hikes, which is bullish for Dell's AI-server and infrastructure franchise. The stock also rode the hardware and semis bounce after Monday's selloff. Cooler CPI added a tailwind.

stocks.name: Onto Innovation
stocks.explanation: Onto Innovation rebounded with semiconductor-equipment names as the group bounced on cooler CPI and firm memory-pricing dynamics. The metrology and inspection supplier is leveraged to advanced-packaging and HBM demand for AI chips. The move recovered ground lost in Monday's chip selloff.

stocks.name: Arm Holdings
stocks.explanation: Arm Holdings fell as investors locked in profits after a roughly 179% year-to-date run, part of a rotation out of richly valued AI and semiconductor names. Adding a company-specific overhang, concerns grew around supply-chain bottlenecks for Arm's newly launched AGI CPU, where hyperscaler demand has outpaced wafer, memory and packaging capacity. Earnings are due July 29.
